title: Company Formation Services in Vietnam
metaDescription: Set up your company in Vietnam with ease. We handle every step of the company formation process — from registration to compliance — so you can focus on growth.

hero.title: Company Formation Services in Vietnam
hero.subtitle: Setting up a business in Vietnam can be challenging, but it doesn't have to be. From representative office setup to complete company registration, we handle the entire company establishment process for you.

sections.heading: Open a company in Vietnam with the help of our registration consultants
body: Expanding your business into a new region can be complex. In Vietnam, navigating registration and administrative requirements is crucial for establishing your company legally and ensuring long-term success.

At InCorp, we've assisted global companies from Asia Pacific, Europe, and North America in smoothly setting up operations in Vietnam. Our experienced team makes your business setup seamless — from registration and compliance to outsourcing and secretarial services — so you can focus on growing your business.

sections.heading: Choose the right company formation option

items.title: Limited Liability Company
items.desc: A flexible, protected entity for all investors — easy to set up and raise capital with minimal paperwork.

items.title: Representative Office
items.desc: A foreign-owned office for market research and hiring, with no capital requirement.

items.title: Branch Office
items.desc: A foreign branch operating under the parent company's structure, able to acquire permits and do business.

items.title: Joint-Stock Company (JSC)
items.desc: A shareholder-based company with transferable shares and limited liability.

sections.heading: Frequently asked questions on opening a company in Vietnam

items.q: Can I set up a company in Vietnam as a foreigner?
items.a: Yes. Foreign investors can establish a foreign-owned company in Vietnam. The most common type is an LLC (Limited Liability Company), known for its straightforward structure and quick setup. The right structure depends on your business type.

items.q: What are the company types available in Vietnam?
items.a: You can open a new company as one of four entities: Limited Liability Company (LLC), Joint-stock Company (JSC), Branch Office, and Representative Office.

items.q: Is there any minimum capital requirements for Vietnam company formation?
items.a: While Vietnamese law does not specify a minimum, having around US$ 25,000–30,000 is typically advised to ensure smooth incorporation and operations.

items.q: Will I be able to hold 100-percent ownership of a foreign-owned company?
items.a: Yes. Vietnamese law enables foreigners to open 100% foreign-owned companies in most sectors, with only a few restricted business sectors.

items.q: Is there any option to expand my business without incorporating a company in Vietnam?
items.a: Yes. Several options suit investors who wish to expand without forming a legal entity, such as a representative office or appointing a local distributor.

items.q: I want to set up a company as soon as possible, is there a way to speed up the incorporation process?
items.a: Setting up an LLC or JSC typically takes 40–60 days. To expedite, consider purchasing a shelf company from InCorp, allowing management control to be transferred to you within a few working days.

items.q: What are the benefits of a local nominee company and a shelf company?
items.a: A local nominee company lets you operate in sectors that require local ownership while keeping management control, whereas a shelf company is a ready-made, pre-registered entity that can be transferred to you quickly so you can start operating almost immediately.

items.q: I would like to set up a joint venture local company, but I do not have a reliable local partner to act as a shareholder. What should I do?
items.a: InCorp can act as a trusted local nominee shareholder on your behalf, allowing you to meet local ownership requirements while retaining management control of the company through a clear legal agreement.

items.q: Does InCorp offer market entry and corporate services outside Vietnam?
items.a: Yes. As part of InCorp Group, we provide market entry, incorporation, and corporate services across the Asia Pacific region, helping you expand into multiple markets through a single partner.
